title: make binary-local for 0.1.21 fails on amd64/linux with go 1.8.3
body:
Here is what I get when I run "make binary-local":

```
go build -ldflags "-X main.gitCommit=0b7315460126b8945fd53f9318a65d7cc8796af1" -gcflags "" -tags " " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
# github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/glibobject
vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/glibobject/gobject.go:69: cannot use v.native() (type *C.struct__GObject) as type C.gpointer in argument to func literal
vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/glibobject/gobject.go:73: cannot use v.native() (type *C.struct__GObject) as type C.gpointer in argument to func literal
make: *** [Makefile:60: binary-local] Error 2

```

comments.body: This is exactly what I am seeing on Fedora 25.
@giuseppe PTAL
Ryan created a PR already in ostree go bindings to fix this.
Link?
https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pull/6

I applied the patch from the previously mentioned p/r and make binary-local fails with a different message:

```
go build -ldflags "-X main.gitCommit=b40a260b1bd1b9334a6aeafbcbdae91921d6e783" -gcflags "" -tags " " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
# pkg-config --cflags ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
pkg-config: exit status 1
make: *** [Makefile:60: binary-local] Error 2

```

Yeah, the `README.md` files (in both repositories) need updating for the added dependency.  Can you verify that one of
```sh
Fedora$ dnf install ostree-devel
Debian$ apt-get install -y libostree-dev
```
fixes this, please? (Reportedly the Ubuntu `libostree-dev` is only present in Zesty, not earlier versions.)

I'm not on either of those distros (I use Gentoo), so I can't verify this.
I checked and libostree is not present on Gentoo.
Might need to have a way to compile out the ostree support then.

I looked at packaging ostree, and something is unclear to me about it. It's not clear from its README whether it requires systemd, which is what their releases page seems to say. Since systemd is optional for us, and since I'm not sure whether ostree requires systemd, I tend to agree that making ostree optional would probably be a good idea.

Well, unless you want to use the ostree backend (which is not the case right now), the dependency is on libostree being _compiled_, not on it _working_. So a compile-time dependency on systemd might not be a show-stopper (unless the distribution requires the systemd runtime to be installed in order to have the build environment available).

We do need a way to make this optional, though. I’m almost tempted to put autoconf in there… Only if it didn't require `autoreconf` after a git checkout.

So, we need a build tag in containers/image which replaces `ostree` in `alltransports` with a dummy “this transport is no available” placeholder, and skopeo’s `Makefile` to use pkg-confing and auto-detect whether ostree is available, configuring the build tag as necessary.

Yes, the distro does need systemd installed in order to compile against it. Gentoo does have systemd, but it is optional, so if someone wants to use the systemd support, they can.

I recommend giving us a way to control at build time whether the systend support is included. For example, give us a setting to pass to the make command to control it.

> Might need to have a way to compile out the ostree support then.

https://github.com/containers/image/pull/290 ; if that works, skopeo can be build with a `BUILDTAGS=`…, or `Makefile` could even auto-detect ostree presence if anyone cared to implement that.
I would support a BUILDTAGS option; that would make it possible for distros to turn off the requirement for systemd. I prefer this over autodetection.
OK, that’s even easier. Can you verify that https://github.com/containers/image/pull/290 works for you, please?
I am unable to get the recommended pull request to apply against skopeo.
I found the problem. The referenced pull request contains a patch to transports/alltransports/alltransports_test.go which does not exist in the vendored copy of the library.
I skipped the patch I referred to in the previous comment, and yes this allows skopeo to be built without systemd.

After https://github.com/containers/image/pull/290 it will be possible to build with
```sh
make binary-local BUILDTAGS=containers_image_ostree_stub
```

It would probably make sense to teach `Makefile` to detect `libostree` presence and to add this tag tag automatically (with a warning?)
I believe this is fixed in skopeo.  Closing, Reopen if I am mistaken.
